prose:
Frontier-orbital calculation on ethylene (C₂H₄, 6 atoms) using DFT at the B3LYP/def2-TZVP level (PySCF RKS, tier 'standard'), gas phase, charge 0, multiplicity 1. Density fitting was OFF (exact four-center integrals). Geometry taken as-is from the user-supplied .xyz file (single-point; no re-optimization). SCF converged in 6 cycles. No warnings reported. integrity.trustworthy = true.

Headline result — HOMO–LUMO gap: 7.4357165 eV
HOMO (index 7): −7.6155818 eV
LUMO (index 8): −0.17986527 eV
Total energy: −78.623486 Hartree (−2139.4541 eV)

Koopmans-derived properties: vertical IP = 7.6155818 eV, vertical EA = 0.17986527 eV, electronegativity χ = 3.8977235 eV, chemical hardness η = 3.7178583 eV, chemical softness S = 0.26897206 eV⁻¹, electrophilicity index ω = 2.0431452 eV.

This is a Koopmans-style orbital gap, not an excitation energy or optical gap. No experimental comparison is offered.
